title: Coryphantha neomexicana
intro: Coryphantha neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico pincushion cactus, is a small, globular cactus species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce oxidative stress. Coryphantha neomexicana contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, including neocardenolide, a cardiac glycoside that has been shown to exhibit cardiotonic activity. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including capsules, tinctures, and teas, to promote cardiovascular health and well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Coryphantha neomexicana is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This plant is commonly classified as a type of pincushion cactus due to its unique, globular shape and short, fine spines. It is further classified within the genus Coryphantha, which contains several species of pincushion cacti native to the Americas. The specific classification of Coryphantha neomexicana is as follows: Coryphantha neomexicana (Engelm.) Britton & Rose (synonyms include Mammillaria neomexicana and Echinocactus neomexicanus). This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Cactaceae family.
uses: Coryphantha neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico pincushion cactus, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its sap is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential to combat certain diseases, including cancer and HIV. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential applications. The traditional use of Coryphantha neomexicana highlights the importance of further investigation into the therapeutic potential of this and other cacti species.

constituents: Coryphantha neomexicana contains active constituents such as alkaloids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties.
